title: helianthemum oelandicum subsp. allionii
intro: Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. allionii, also known as rockrose or Allion's rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. allionii include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal medicine to create salves and ointments for topical application.
